what_it_does: Adds CSS presentation rules for a 'Composition workspace' UI: header, rendered specimen stage, switchable variants, collapsible variable rows, resolution-source pills, value trace tiers, instance list, generate row, and memory panel. It relies on decisions.css for the underlying grid/master/detail/row layout.
observations: Token-driven throughout, using CSS custom properties for color, spacing, radius, shadow, and typography.
Layout is explicitly additive only; grid/master/detail/rows/back classes (.dsys__*) are reused from decisions.css.
Source pills (.comp-src--*) map to a resolution order: explicit, preference, memory, ai, default, unresolved.
Variable rows are native <details>/<summary> elements with custom grid layout and hidden default markers.
Each variable exposes a trace (.comp-var__trace) of tiered resolution candidates with chosen/muted states.
Memory panel uses origin pills: approved, rejected, stated.
Shape icon wrappers (.comp-head__ico--shape, .dsys__row-ico--shape) deliberately remove background/border to avoid double rendering.
Mobile breakpoint (max-width: 640px) collapses variable type/value columns and trace values.
Variant picker uses an .on class to highlight the selected thumbnail with a gold border and soft shadow.
Generate row references shared CandidateCard styling from identity.css.
standouts: Resolution-source pill taxonomy tied to MS_RESOLVE.ORDER.
Native <details> used for collapsible, auditable variable rows with tiered traces.
Memory-origin badges that surface approved/rejected/stated user decisions.
Shape-wrapper override to prevent double fill/border behind EntityShape.
conventions: Heavy use of CSS custom-property tokens for theming.
Additive, file-local scope declared in the header comment.
Semantic status tokens: status-error, status-success, status-info, accent-gold, accent-bronze.
Native HTML elements (<details>/<summary>) with default markers suppressed.
Mobile-first-ish responsive via max-width media query.
concerns: Mix of magic numbers (44px, 200px, 168px, 60px, 26px, 20px) alongside token values.
No visible focus or active styles for .comp-variant__pick or .comp-variant__decide buttons.
.comp-src--ai and .comp-src--default share identical colors, which may confuse distinction.
Mobile layout hides .comp-var__type and .comp-var__val without an accessible alternative.
.comp-memory uses a light gold background that may not meet contrast expectations depending on token values.
No dark-mode tokens or media queries visible in this file.
notes: This is the skin for a design-token composition/debug view, built to show where each variable value came from and what the user has already approved or rejected.
questions: What exact markup is expected inside .comp-variant__pick?
How is the .on class toggled for variants?
What is the expected CandidateCard markup referenced from identity.css?
Does this file support a dark mode, or are dark tokens defined elsewhere?
Where are variables such as --accent-gold-50 and --accent-gold-dashed defined?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it adds composition-specific styling for variable resolution and memory while reusing layout from decisions.css.
apparent_themes: Auditability and provenance of design-token values.
User memory and explicit approval/rejection of choices.
intention_signals: It appears designed to make the origin and resolution history of every design variable visible and editable, while surfacing prior user decisions as first-class UI elements.
novelty: The explicit mapping of CSS source-pill classes to a named resolution order (MS_RESOLVE.ORDER) and the tiered 'explain trace' pattern for variable provenance.
contribution: A self-contained, token-driven set of composition UI rules—particularly the variable provenance pills, collapsible trace tiers, and memory-origin badges—that a fused system could retain for auditing design decisions.
summary_for_embedding: CSS rules for a composition workspace that renders design specimens, lists resolvable variables with source-provenance pills and tiered traces, and surfaces user memory of approved or rejected choices.
intention_for_embedding: To style an inspectable composition interface where designers can see why a token value was chosen and review prior approvals and rejections.
